summary: Quadruped trunk + leg geometry implementation passes all acceptance criteria with one minor visual deviation.

checks.geometry_properties: bodyLength, bodyWidth, bodyHeight, legLength1, legLength2, shoulderWidth (lines 3-9)

buildGeometry.note: Upper leg segments connect from body box BOTTOM corners (z=-bodyHeight/2) to knees, not from shoulder positions at z=0 as specified in ADR-0005 §4. The body vertices used are [3,2,4,1] (bottom corners) instead of shoulder positions [±bx, ±sw, 0]. Visually minor — indices are valid and the wireframe renders correctly.

checks.full_6DOF: position, quaternion, linear vel, angular vel (lines 132-191)
checks.foot_velocity_world: R * (vel + cross(omega, foot_body)) (line 158) — correct, handles linear+angular

checks.all_params_specified: bodyLength, bodyWidth, bodyHeight, legLength1, legLength2, shoulderWidth, mass, inertia, k_contact, b_contact, mu

checks.demo_m_existing_cases: DifferentialDrive (lines 9-15) and Quadcopter (lines 16-23) cases present and unchanged

issues.description: Leg edge start points use body box bottom corner vertices instead of shoulder positions. ADR-0005 specifies shoulders at [±bodyLength/2, ±shoulderWidth, 0] (z=0, mid-body), but edges connect from bottom corners [±bx, ±by, -bz] (z=-bodyHeight/2). The 4 leg edges should start from shoulder vertices (not in body vertex set) or from body top corners for a more faithful representation.
issues.recommendation: Either add shoulder vertices to the vertex array and reference them in leg edges, or use body top vertices 5-8 (at z=+bz) as starting points for leg segments instead of bottom vertices 1-4.
